assertions.title: Default Values Usage

steps.observed: Preset created with parameters having default values and required=false
NO_CONNECTION error - cannot verify default usage without database connection

assertions.issues: Cannot verify default value usage without a database connection. The connection error occurs before query execution.

assertions.title: Default Values Override

steps.observed: Preset created successfully

steps.action: Run preset with explicit param value
steps.observed: NO_CONNECTION error - cannot verify override without database connection

assertions.issues: Cannot verify default value override without a database connection.

assertions.title: Default Values for All Types

steps.action: Create preset with defaults for string, int, date, datetime, bool, list
steps.observed: String, int, date, bool, list defaults stored correctly. Datetime has parsing issue with ':' character
All types show default field with correct values (datetime partially correct)

assertions.issues: Datetime default parsing has a bug: '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z' is incorrectly parsed because ':' is used as delimiter in --param-def format. The default value is truncated at '2024-01-15T10' and '30' becomes the description.

steps.action: Create preset with parameters without defaults
steps.observed: Parameters without defaults are marked required=true (not optional)
Optional = has default (required=false), Required = no default (required=true)

steps.action: Create preset with required_param (no default) and optional_param (with default)
steps.expected: required_param has required=true, optional_param has required=false
steps.observed: Correct - required_param has required=true, optional_param has required=false with default
MISSING_PARAMETER error with clear message listing missing params
NO_CONNECTION error (validation passed, no MISSING_PARAMETER)

steps.action: Create preset with optional param having default
steps.expected: Parameter stored with required=false and default value
steps.observed: Correct - optional_param has required=false and default='default_value'

steps.expected: Default would be used (blocked by no connection)
steps.observed: NO_CONNECTION error - no MISSING_PARAMETER error, indicating validation passed

steps.expected: Error with param name, type, and example
steps.observed: Error: MISSING_PARAMETER with message 'missing required parameters: required_param' and hint 'Provide values: --param required_param=value'

steps.action: Provide non-numeric value for int param
steps.expected: Error with param name, expected type, received type, and value
steps.observed: Error: TYPE_MISMATCH with message 'parameter "int_param": value is not a valid integer (expected type: int, received: "not_a_number")'

steps.action: Provide float value for int param
steps.expected: Clear error about float not allowed
steps.observed: Error: TYPE_MISMATCH with message 'parameter "int_param": value is a float, expected an integer (no decimal point allowed) (expected type: int, received: "3.14")'
Error: TYPE_MISMATCH with message 'parameter "date_param": invalid date format, expected YYYY-MM-DD (expected type: date, received: "invalid-date")'
Error: TYPE_MISMATCH with message 'parameter "bool_param": invalid boolean value, accepted: true/false, TRUE/FALSE, 1/0, yes/no (expected type: bool, received: "invalid_bool")'

steps.action: Provide numeric string '123' for int param
steps.observed: No TYPE_MISMATCH error - conversion successful
No TYPE_MISMATCH error - validation passed
All accepted without TYPE_MISMATCH error

frictions.description: Datetime default parsing in --param-def flag uses ':' as delimiter, causing datetime values with 'T' separator (e.g., 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z) to be incorrectly parsed
frictions.resolution: Could not resolve - this is a bug in the CLI argument parsing

blockers.description: No Protheus database connection available - cannot test actual query execution with parameter substitution
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked for connection profiles using 'connection list' - none registered

summary: Tested 9 assertions: 6 passed, 2 blocked (no database connection), 1 passed with issue (datetime default parsing bug). Key findings: (1) Parameters with defaults are automatically optional (required=false), without defaults are required (required=true). (2) Error messages are clear and actionable with proper codes (MISSING_PARAMETER, TYPE_MISMATCH). (3) Type validation works correctly for int, date, bool types. (4) Automatic type conversion works for numeric strings to int. (5) Boolean normalization accepts true/false, TRUE/FALSE, 1/0, yes/no. (6) Bug: datetime defaults with ':' characters are incorrectly parsed due to delimiter conflict.
